title: Do not print unnecessary Buildah details during commit
body:
Noticed this while investigating #705 

We should never print straight to stdout in libpod. We need to either accept a writer for reports, or not print at all. The latter seems easiest, and this comment doesn't seem particularly valuable.

comments.body: @TomSweeneyRedHat I'd be fine changing it to accept a separate writer to print the ID, too. This seemed less invasive.
@TomSweeneyRedHat to be clear, this doesn't remove the output, it just moves it into `cmd/buildah/commit.go` where Podman won't hit it.

Wait, I'm just getting really confused as to which PR I'm commenting on...
@TomSweeneyRedHat Can we print this to stderr instead? Would that break anything?

Now I'm confuzled @mheon.  Printing to stderr is OK in general, I'm just a little leery if folks try scripting stuff up and want to get data back from stdout.  My confusion at this point with this change is where is the progress output going from the commit?  Is that still being printed?  I thought this change shut it off in libpod land.

@TomSweeneyRedHat Why would we want to capture the buildah comment in a script? I can see grabbing the image ID once we're done (we print that in `cmd/podman/commit.go`) but this seems purely like internal details on what buildah did?

:pushpin: Commit 7a38683 has been approved by `rhatdan`
